title: Platform Seeing: Image Ensembles and Their Invisualities
author: MacKenzie, Munster

opening_move.description: The paper opens by diagnosing that contemporary visual culture has become imperceptible and automated, such that existing frameworks for understanding vision (observation, representation, subjectivity) cannot account for the distributed, platform-mediated operations of image ensembles. The puzzle is: how can one 'see' what platform seeing does if it is by definition invisual?
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Understanding the reorganization of contemporary forms of knowing and perceiving requires new conceptual tools that go beyond both algorithmic culture critique and machine vision discourse.

theoretical_positioning.description: The paper positions itself against both dystopian algorithmic culture critique and representation-centred visual theory, proposing instead an ensemble-ontological account of platform seeing drawn from Bergson and Deleuze-Guattari.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Techno-philosophical close reading of AI architectures (AlphaGo, Q-networks, DeepMind, iPhone ISPs, GPU architectures) as cases for theorising platform seeing; Bergsonian ontological reconstruction applied to contemporary technical objects.
epistemic_mode.notes: The paper uses specific technical cases (AlphaGo, smartphone ISPs, GPU architectures) as interpretive objects — not as empirical evidence in a social-scientific sense, but as material through which the conceptual argument about invisuality is demonstrated. The cases illustrate and instantiate the theory rather than test or constitute it.

case_type.case_name: AlphaGo, DeepMind image operations, iPhone/ARM ISPs, GPU architectures

confidence_notes: The epistemic mode is a borderline case: the paper uses technical cases (AI systems, hardware) as lenses through which to build theory, but these are not cultural objects in the conventional sense (film, literary text). Classified as normative_with_interpretive_case because the cases function as philosophical-material parables, not as empirical data. The normative stake is explicitly low — the paper makes no prescriptive claims, its diagnostic ambition is methodological-conceptual rather than political. Move sequence involves two parallel rounds of case_introduction/development/interpretation (DeepMind/AlphaGo, then devices/smartphones, then GPU architecture), collapsed here for legibility.
